summary: Post-P1 baseline. All required CI jobs are GREEN. Extracted-block jobs (shellcheck-extracted, bandit-extracted) are informational and the documented findings are grandfathered until TUNE-0048 tags illustrative blocks with explicit opt-out markers.

required_jobs_status.semgrep: 0 findings (1 finding suppressed inline with documented reason — see suppressions[])
required_jobs_status.osv-scanner: no package sources (no package-lock.json / requirements.txt / etc. in scope)

shellcheck-extracted.rationale: shipped skills contain illustrative snippets with <PLACEHOLDER> syntax that is not valid bash; these are documentation, not standalone scripts.
shellcheck-extracted.promotion_path: TUNE-0048 — tag each illustrative block with `# nosec-extract` or wrap in `<!-- security:counter-example -->` fence; flip continue-on-error: false.

bandit-extracted.skipped_reason: syntax error while parsing AST from doc snippet (not real Python code)

suppressions.reason: canonical strict-mode IFS, not derived from input — defensive, not vulnerable
suppressions.marker: # nosemgrep: bash.lang.security.ifs-tampering.ifs-tampering -- canonical strict-mode IFS, not derived from input

follow_up_tasks.title: Tag illustrative bash/python blocks in shipped docs to promote shellcheck-extracted + bandit-extracted to required CI jobs
follow_up_tasks.trigger: before next major framework release (1.19.x or 2.0.0)
follow_up_tasks.estimate: 2-3 hours sweep over skills/, agents/, commands/, templates/, docs/
